## vvx
Job Jockey Kit - MCP tools for project initiative management

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) on a cron schedule or once at a specific time. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- rbk-prep-release: Prepare release candidate for upstream delivery
- vvc-BREAK-LOCK: Force-break a stuck VVC lock after a crash.
- vvc-commit: Guarded git commit
- cma-prep-pr: Prepare branch for upstream PR contribution
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Next: jettison-multi-platform-cascade-fix (₢BAAAG) [rough]

Docket:
  ## Character
  
  Targeted machinery fix on a defect surfaced empirically by ₣A_'s AAS pace.
  Small-diff, high-confidence change once the cascade traversal is written —
  the OCI Distribution Spec already nails down the index-to-children
  relationship, and `zrbfc_gar_extract_artifact` (rbfc_FoundryCore.sh:575)
  already implements the read-side of that traversal. This pace mirrors that
  traversal on the delete side.
  
  The risk surface is narrow: existing single-platform jettisons (pouch on
  bind, anything else not pushed via buildx) must remain unchanged. The new
  code path activates only when the manifest at the locator is an OCI image
  index / Docker manifest list.
  
  Verification is concrete: the batch_vouch fixture (already implemented under
  AAS) is the regression test. It currently FAILS at step 03 because vouch
  jettison leaves the package container in GAR's ListPackages; after this pace
  lands, it must pass step 03 (tally pending) through step 06 (abjure) cleanly.
  
  ## Docket
  
  ### Empirical motivation (recorded for the next mounting)
  
  `rbfl_jettison` (rbfl_FoundryLedger.sh:328+) calls Docker Registry V2
  `DELETE /v2/<pkg>/manifests/<tag>`. For single-platform pushes (e.g. pouch,
  which is `docker push` from rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh:734), this removes
  the only manifest, the package becomes empty, and GAR drops it from
  `ListPackages`. For multi-platform buildx pushes (vouch in
  rbgjv03-assemble-push-vouch.sh:48-52, all reliquary tool images, all
  enshrinements), the tag points to an OCI image index referencing N
  per-platform manifests; DELETE-by-tag removes only the index, leaving the N
  children as untagged content. The package container persists in
  `ListPackages` because it still has manifests.
  
  `zrbfc_list_hallmarks_capture` (rbfc_FoundryCore.sh:531) and consequently
  `rbfl_tally` and `rbfl_rekon` enumerate via `ListPackages`, so they continue
  to report the basename present after a jettison-by-tag against multi-platform
  content. AAS's batch_vouch fixture exposed this as a tally-still-vouched
  failure post-jettison; the morning's stale-hallmark abjure (which uses the
  GAR REST package-delete path, not the V2 manifest path) cleanly removed all
  six packages including vouch — confirming the package container persistence
  is real and is exactly what abjure already overcomes.
  
  The asymmetry was bind_lifecycle's pouch jettison passing while batch_vouch's
  vouch jettison failed; same locator grammar, different push mechanism on the
  producer side.
  
  ### Approach: cascade through the index
  
  In `rbfl_jettison`, after parsing the locator and authenticating, fetch the
  manifest at `<pkg>/manifests/<tag>`. Branch on mediaType:
  
  1. **Single manifest** (`application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.v2+json`,
     `application/vnd.oci.image.manifest.v1+json`): existing behavior —
     DELETE-by-tag, done.
  2. **Image index / manifest list** (`application/vnd.oci.image.index.v1+json`,
     `application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.list.v2+json`): enumerate
     `.manifests[].digest`. For each child digest, DELETE
     `<pkg>/manifests/<digest>`. After all children are removed, DELETE
     `<pkg>/manifests/<tag>` (the index itself) to clear the tag reference.
  
  Mirror `zrbfc_gar_extract_artifact`'s mediaType branch (rbfc_FoundryCore.sh:622)
  for the recognition pattern; that function already handles the same
  branching shape on the read side.
  
  ### Surface
  
  - **`Tools/rbk/rbfl_FoundryLedger.sh`** — extend `rbfl_jettison` to do the
    cascade. Most lines added are inside the existing function; helpers
    factored into `rbfc_FoundryCore.sh` if they generalize to other future
    callers (likely just one helper: enumerate-and-delete-children-by-digest).
  - **`Tools/rbk/rbfc_FoundryCore.sh`** — possibly add a small helper
    `zrbfc_jettison_index_children` if extraction is clean. If the cascade
    fits cleanly inline, skip the extraction.
  
  No tabtarget surface change. No spec change.
  
  ### Spec touch
  
  - **`Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBSIJ-image_jettison.adoc`** — current spec
    describes tag-by-tag DELETE without acknowledging the multi-platform case.
    Add a note (post-cascade): "Multi-platform manifests (OCI image indices /
    manifest lists) are jettisoned by enumerating per-platform child manifests
    via the index, deleting each by digest, then deleting the index by tag.
    This produces a fully-empty package, which GAR then drops from
    `ListPackages`."
  
  ### Verification
  
  1. **Regression — batch_vouch fixture must pass end-to-end:**
     `tt/rbtd-s.SingleCase.batch-vouch.sh rbtdrc_batch_vouch_lifecycle`
     (currently fails at step 03 tally pending; after this pace must pass
     through step 07 passed).
  2. **Bind/pouch unaffected:** `tt/rbtd-s.SingleCase.four-mode.sh
     rbtdrc_fourmode_bind_lifecycle` — single-platform jettison path must
     still pass.
  3. **All four single-mode SingleCases GREEN** — sanity sweep:
     conjure_lifecycle, bind_lifecycle, graft_lifecycle, kludge_lifecycle.
  4. **Fast suite GREEN** — no regression on non-crucible tests
     (`tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh`).
  5. **Manual one-shot:** ordain a fresh hallmark, jettison vouch via
     `rbw-iJh.DirectorJettisonsHallmarkImage.sh`, run `rbw-ft` —
     the hallmark should reflect "pending" (image+about, no vouch) rather than
     "vouched" with all six basenames.
  6. **Cost discipline:** the regression test is one ordain (≈3-5min Cloud
     Build) per fixture run. Don't loop unnecessarily.
  
  ### Hard dependency notes
  
  - `₢BAAAF` (imageops-fixtures) **depends on this pace landing**. BAAAF's
    `muster_absent` cases assert concrete absence, which requires the cascade
    fix to make packages truly empty.
  - This pace is independent of `₢BAAAA` (oauth hygiene), `₢BAAAC` (OCI URL
    keyword extraction), and `₢BAAAB` (BCG shellcheck sweep) — disjoint
    surfaces.
  
  ### Out of scope
  
  - **Changing `rbfl_abjure`.** Abjure already uses GAR REST package-delete
    and works correctly for multi-platform content; don't refactor it.
  - **Changing `zrbfc_list_hallmarks_capture` semantics.** Package-level
    enumeration is correct given the post-cascade behavior. Don't switch to
    tag-level enumeration.
  - **Adding new tabtargets.** The existing `rbw-iJh` /
    `DirectorJettisonsHallmarkImage` (and its sibling `rbw-iJr` / `rbw-iJe`
    introduced by BAAAD) suffice; the cascade is a behavior change inside
    `rbfl_jettison`, not a new operation.
  - **Changing the producer side** (rbgjv03's buildx push, rbgjb*'s buildx
    pushes, enshrine's skopeo copy). Multi-platform pushes are a feature, not
    a defect; the consumer side (jettison) just needs to handle them.
  - **Cascading abjure semantics into jettison** (i.e., letting jettison
    delete a whole package, not a tag). The spec for jettison is "by locator"
    not "by package"; broadening would conflate operations.
  - **Backward-compat shims** for the silent-no-op behavior. Hard cutover:
    after this pace, jettison-by-tag against a multi-platform locator does
    the right thing; nothing should depend on the broken behavior.
  
  ### Cadence
  
  1. Mount BA, parade this pace, read this docket.
  2. Read `rbfl_FoundryLedger.sh` (jettison function), `rbfc_FoundryCore.sh`
     (zrbfc_gar_extract_artifact for the mediaType branch shape), and
     `rbgjv03-assemble-push-vouch.sh` (confirm buildx push shape) before
     any edit.
  3. Implement the cascade. Single notch when:
     - bash -n / shellcheck clean on touched files
     - bind_lifecycle GREEN (no regression)
     - batch_vouch GREEN (the regression test passes)
  4. RBSIJ note update can ride the same notch or a follow-up — choice at
     the working chat's discretion.
  5. Wrap.
  
  ### References
  
  - ₣A_'s AAS docket and wrap summary (commit `3a9199ba` + chain) — full
    empirical investigation and decision history.
  - `Tools/rbk/rbfc_FoundryCore.sh:575` (`zrbfc_gar_extract_artifact`) —
    read-side mediaType branch, the structural template for the delete-side
    cascade.
  - `Tools/rbk/rbfl_FoundryLedger.sh:328` (`rbfl_jettison`) — the function
    to extend.
  - `Tools/rbk/rbgjv/rbgjv03-assemble-push-vouch.sh:48-52` — confirms vouch
    is a multi-platform buildx push (canonical example).
  - `Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBSIJ-image_jettison.adoc` — spec to amend.

31	Small-diff, high-confidence change once the cascade traversal is written —

49	### Empirical motivation (recorded for the next mounting)
50	
51	`rbfl_jettison` (rbfl_FoundryLedger.sh:328+) calls Docker Registry V2
52	`DELETE /v2/<pkg>/manifests/<tag>`. For single-platform pushes (e.g. pouch,
53	which is `docker push` from rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh:734), this removes
54	the only manifest, the package becomes empty, and GAR drops it from
55	`ListPackages`. For multi-platform buildx pushes (vouch in
56	rbgjv03-assemble-push-vouch.sh:48-52, all reliquary tool images, all
57	enshrinements), the tag points to an OCI image index referencing N
58	per-platform manifests; DELETE-by-tag removes only the index, leaving the N
59	children as untagged content. The package container persists in

70

72	vouch jettison failed; same locator grammar, different push mechanism on the
73	producer side.
74

76	
77	In `rbfl_jettison`, after parsing the locator and authenticating, fetch the
78	manifest at `<pkg>/manifests/<tag>`. Branch on mediaType:
79	
80	1. **Single manifest** (`application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.v2+json`,
81	   `application/vnd.oci.image.manifest.v1+json`): existing behavior —
82	   DELETE-by-tag, done.
83	2. **Image index / manifest list** (`application/vnd.oci.image.index.v1+json`,
84	   `application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.list.v2+json`): enumerate
85	   `.manifests[].digest`. For each child digest, DELETE
86	   `<pkg>/manifests/<digest>`. After all children are removed, DELETE
87	   `<pkg>/manifests/<tag>` (the index itself) to clear the tag reference.
88	
89	Mirror `zrbfc_gar_extract_artifact`'s mediaType branch (rbfc_FoundryCore.sh:622)

105	### Spec touch

107	- **`Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBSIJ-image_jettison.adoc`** — current spec
108	  describes tag-by-tag DELETE without acknowledging the multi-platform case.
109	  Add a note (post-cascade): "Multi-platform manifests (OCI image indices /
110	  manifest lists) are jettisoned by enumerating per-platform child manifests
111	  via the index, deleting each by digest, then deleting the index by tag.
112	  This produces a fully-empty package, which GAR then drops from

124	3. **All four single-mode SingleCases GREEN** — sanity sweep:

126	4. **Fast suite GREEN** — no regression on non-crucible tests

138	  `muster_absent` cases assert concrete absence, which requires the cascade
139	  fix to make packages truly empty.
140	- This pace is independent of `₢BAAAA` (oauth hygiene), `₢BAAAC` (OCI URL
141	  keyword extraction), and `₢BAAAB` (BCG shellcheck sweep) — disjoint
142	  surfaces.
143	
144	### Out of scope
145	
146	- **Changing `rbfl_abjure`.** Abjure already uses GAR REST package-delete
147	  and works correctly for multi-platform content; don't refactor it.
148	- **Changing `zrbfc_list_hallmarks_capture` semantics.** Package-level

## Context

Sibling heat to ₣A_ (rbk-mvp-3-resource-prefix-and-depot-regen). Holds the post-AAK paces whose work is independent of AAE depot regeneration, so they can race in parallel to ₣A_'s live-infra spine. Intended execution pattern: a second officium working in `../rbm_beta_recipemuster` while the primary officium continues ₣A_ in the main tree.

All design decisions are baked into individual dockets — agents executing here should expect mechanical-apply work with explicit file lists and verbatim verification gates.

## Cross-officium discipline

This heat shares the repo with ₣A_; the parallel-tree split is geometric, not semantic. File-level boundaries enforce coordination — a pace's docket may carry an explicit blacklist of files belonging to ₣A_'s active paces, and those constraints persist across directories. When in doubt, additive only: no destructive git operations, no fixing "wrong" repo state without asking, commits land only the file lists each docket specifies.

## Test-suite reservation

During the parallel period, this heat runs fast suite only. Crucible, service, and complete suites are reserved for ₣A_'s live-infra paces — they share regime state and container/network namespaces, and concurrent runs will fail. Once ₣A_'s burn-in clears (or this heat's tail crosses with ₣A_'s wrap), the restriction can lift.

## Live-GCP coordination

Most paces in this heat are pure local refactor or spec churn — no GCP cost. Where a docket does call for live infrastructure, it carries explicit cost notes. Coordinate any billable runs against ₣A_'s active spend; ₣A_ owns the heavier live-infra workload during the parallel period.

## References

- Parent heat ₣A_ paddock — design history for resource prefixing (`RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX`, `RBRR_RUNTIME_PREFIX`), GAR categorical layout migration (hallmarks/reliquaries/enshrines), payor subdir migration. Vocabulary established there is assumed background.
- BCG (`Tools/buk/vov_veiled/BCG-BashConsoleGuide.md`) — referenced for cross-module shellcheck discipline study.

# jjezs_pace ₢BAAAG

## Character

Targeted machinery fix on a defect surfaced empirically by ₣A_'s AAS pace.
Small-diff, high-confidence change once the cascade traversal is written —
the OCI Distribution Spec already nails down the index-to-children
relationship, and `zrbfc_gar_extract_artifact` (rbfc_FoundryCore.sh:575)
already implements the read-side of that traversal. This pace mirrors that
traversal on the delete side.

The risk surface is narrow: existing single-platform jettisons (pouch on
bind, anything else not pushed via buildx) must remain unchanged. The new
code path activates only when the manifest at the locator is an OCI image
index / Docker manifest list.

Verification is concrete: the batch_vouch fixture (already implemented under
AAS) is the regression test. It currently FAILS at step 03 because vouch
jettison leaves the package container in GAR's ListPackages; after this pace
lands, it must pass step 03 (tally pending) through step 06 (abjure) cleanly.

## Docket

### Empirical motivation (recorded for the next mounting)

`rbfl_jettison` (rbfl_FoundryLedger.sh:328+) calls Docker Registry V2
`DELETE /v2/<pkg>/manifests/<tag>`. For single-platform pushes (e.g. pouch,
which is `docker push` from rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh:734), this removes
the only manifest, the package becomes empty, and GAR drops it from
`ListPackages`. For multi-platform buildx pushes (vouch in
rbgjv03-assemble-push-vouch.sh:48-52, all reliquary tool images, all
enshrinements), the tag points to an OCI image index referencing N
per-platform manifests; DELETE-by-tag removes only the index, leaving the N
children as untagged content. The package container persists in
`ListPackages` because it still has manifests.

`zrbfc_list_hallmarks_capture` (rbfc_FoundryCore.sh:531) and consequently
`rbfl_tally` and `rbfl_rekon` enumerate via `ListPackages`, so they continue
to report the basename present after a jettison-by-tag against multi-platform
content. AAS's batch_vouch fixture exposed this as a tally-still-vouched
failure post-jettison; the morning's stale-hallmark abjure (which uses the
GAR REST package-delete path, not the V2 manifest path) cleanly removed all
six packages including vouch — confirming the package container persistence
is real and is exactly what abjure already overcomes.

The asymmetry was bind_lifecycle's pouch jettison passing while batch_vouch's
vouch jettison failed; same locator grammar, different push mechanism on the
producer side.

### Approach: cascade through the index

In `rbfl_jettison`, after parsing the locator and authenticating, fetch the
manifest at `<pkg>/manifests/<tag>`. Branch on mediaType:

1. **Single manifest** (`application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.v2+json`,
   `application/vnd.oci.image.manifest.v1+json`): existing behavior —
   DELETE-by-tag, done.
2. **Image index / manifest list** (`application/vnd.oci.image.index.v1+json`,
   `application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.list.v2+json`): enumerate
   `.manifests[].digest`. For each child digest, DELETE
   `<pkg>/manifests/<digest>`. After all children are removed, DELETE
   `<pkg>/manifests/<tag>` (the index itself) to clear the tag reference.

Mirror `zrbfc_gar_extract_artifact`'s mediaType branch (rbfc_FoundryCore.sh:622)
for the recognition pattern; that function already handles the same
branching shape on the read side.

### Surface

- **`Tools/rbk/rbfl_FoundryLedger.sh`** — extend `rbfl_jettison` to do the
  cascade. Most lines added are inside the existing function; helpers
  factored into `rbfc_FoundryCore.sh` if they generalize to other future
  callers (likely just one helper: enumerate-and-delete-children-by-digest).
- **`Tools/rbk/rbfc_FoundryCore.sh`** — possibly add a small helper
  `zrbfc_jettison_index_children` if extraction is clean. If the cascade
  fits cleanly inline, skip the extraction.

No tabtarget surface change. No spec change.

### Spec touch

- **`Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBSIJ-image_jettison.adoc`** — current spec
  describes tag-by-tag DELETE without acknowledging the multi-platform case.
  Add a note (post-cascade): "Multi-platform manifests (OCI image indices /
  manifest lists) are jettisoned by enumerating per-platform child manifests
  via the index, deleting each by digest, then deleting the index by tag.
  This produces a fully-empty package, which GAR then drops from
  `ListPackages`."

### Verification

1. **Regression — batch_vouch fixture must pass end-to-end:**
   `tt/rbtd-s.SingleCase.batch-vouch.sh rbtdrc_batch_vouch_lifecycle`
   (currently fails at step 03 tally pending; after this pace must pass
   through step 07 passed).
2. **Bind/pouch unaffected:** `tt/rbtd-s.SingleCase.four-mode.sh
   rbtdrc_fourmode_bind_lifecycle` — single-platform jettison path must
   still pass.
3. **All four single-mode SingleCases GREEN** — sanity sweep:
   conjure_lifecycle, bind_lifecycle, graft_lifecycle, kludge_lifecycle.
4. **Fast suite GREEN** — no regression on non-crucible tests
   (`tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh`).
5. **Manual one-shot:** ordain a fresh hallmark, jettison vouch via
   `rbw-iJh.DirectorJettisonsHallmarkImage.sh`, run `rbw-ft` —
   the hallmark should reflect "pending" (image+about, no vouch) rather than
   "vouched" with all six basenames.
6. **Cost discipline:** the regression test is one ordain (≈3-5min Cloud
   Build) per fixture run. Don't loop unnecessarily.

### Hard dependency notes

- `₢BAAAF` (imageops-fixtures) **depends on this pace landing**. BAAAF's
  `muster_absent` cases assert concrete absence, which requires the cascade
  fix to make packages truly empty.
- This pace is independent of `₢BAAAA` (oauth hygiene), `₢BAAAC` (OCI URL
  keyword extraction), and `₢BAAAB` (BCG shellcheck sweep) — disjoint
  surfaces.

### Out of scope

- **Changing `rbfl_abjure`.** Abjure already uses GAR REST package-delete
  and works correctly for multi-platform content; don't refactor it.
- **Changing `zrbfc_list_hallmarks_capture` semantics.** Package-level
  enumeration is correct given the post-cascade behavior. Don't switch to
  tag-level enumeration.
- **Adding new tabtargets.** The existing `rbw-iJh` /
  `DirectorJettisonsHallmarkImage` (and its sibling `rbw-iJr` / `rbw-iJe`
  introduced by BAAAD) suffice; the cascade is a behavior change inside
  `rbfl_jettison`, not a new operation.
- **Changing the producer side** (rbgjv03's buildx push, rbgjb*'s buildx
  pushes, enshrine's skopeo copy). Multi-platform pushes are a feature, not
  a defect; the consumer side (jettison) just needs to handle them.
- **Cascading abjure semantics into jettison** (i.e., letting jettison
  delete a whole package, not a tag). The spec for jettison is "by locator"
  not "by package"; broadening would conflate operations.
- **Backward-compat shims** for the silent-no-op behavior. Hard cutover:
  after this pace, jettison-by-tag against a multi-platform locator does
  the right thing; nothing should depend on the broken behavior.

### Cadence

1. Mount BA, parade this pace, read this docket.
2. Read `rbfl_FoundryLedger.sh` (jettison function), `rbfc_FoundryCore.sh`
   (zrbfc_gar_extract_artifact for the mediaType branch shape), and
   `rbgjv03-assemble-push-vouch.sh` (confirm buildx push shape) before
   any edit.
3. Implement the cascade. Single notch when:
   - bash -n / shellcheck clean on touched files
   - bind_lifecycle GREEN (no regression)
   - batch_vouch GREEN (the regression test passes)
4. RBSIJ note update can ride the same notch or a follow-up — choice at
   the working chat's discretion.
5. Wrap.

### References

- ₣A_'s AAS docket and wrap summary (commit `3a9199ba` + chain) — full
  empirical investigation and decision history.
- `Tools/rbk/rbfc_FoundryCore.sh:575` (`zrbfc_gar_extract_artifact`) —
  read-side mediaType branch, the structural template for the delete-side
  cascade.
- `Tools/rbk/rbfl_FoundryLedger.sh:328` (`rbfl_jettison`) — the function
  to extend.
- `Tools/rbk/rbgjv/rbgjv03-assemble-push-vouch.sh:48-52` — confirms vouch
  is a multi-platform buildx push (canonical example).
- `Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBSIJ-image_jettison.adoc` — spec to amend.

## Assessment: Yes, substantial read-only progress is possible

The docket's cadence step 2 is explicitly read-only and load-bearing:

> Read `rbfl_FoundryLedger.sh` (jettison function), `rbfc_FoundryCore.sh` (zrbfc_gar_extract_artifact for the mediaType branch shape), and `rbgjv03-assemble-push-vouch.sh` (confirm buildx push shape) **before any edit**.

**What we can do without touching the filesystem:**
- Read the three referenced files and trace the actual structure of `rbfl_jettison`, the read-side mediaType branch in `zrbfc_gar_extract_artifact`, and the buildx push shape
- Verify the docket's premise (that the read-side cascade is a clean structural template for the delete-side)
- Draft the exact diff: which lines change in `rbfl_jettison`, whether a `zrbfc_jettison_index_children` helper earns extraction or stays inline, and the precise auth/curl/jq mechanics
- Sketch the RBSIJ note text
- Confirm the out-of-scope boundaries hold (no producer-side touch needed, no tabtarget surface change)

**What requires filesystem changes (deferred):**
- Applying the edit
- Running `bash -n` / shellcheck
- Running `bind_lifecycle` and `batch_vouch` SingleCases (also costs Cloud Build minutes)
- Notching and wrapping

The tradeoff: a read-only pass produces a concrete plan ready to apply in one notch on the next mount, with the design pre-validated against the actual code rather than against the docket's description of it. The risk is that the pre-validation surfaces something the docket missed — at which case we'd want to reslate before applying, which is itself a filesystem op via `jjx_redocket`.

1	#!/bin/bash
2	#
3	# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
4	#
5	# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
6	# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
7	# You may obtain a copy of the License at
8	#
9	#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
10	#
11	# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
12	# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
13	# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
14	# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
15	# limitations under the License.
16	#
17	# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
18	#
19	# Recipe Bottle Foundry Ledger - inscribe, yoke, jettison, abjure, wrest, rekon, muster, and tally operations
20	# Director credentials for inscribe, yoke, jettison, abjure, wrest, rekon, muster; retriever credentials for tally
21	
22	set -euo pipefail
23	
24	# Multiple inclusion detection
25	test -z "${ZRBFL_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbfl multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
26	ZRBFL_SOURCED=1
27	
28	# Source shared Foundry Core module
29	source "${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}/rbfc_FoundryCore.sh"
30	
31	######################################################################
32	# Internal Functions (zrbfl_*)
33	
34	zrbfl_kindle() {
35	  test -z "${ZRBFL_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbfl already kindled"
36	
37	  buc_log_args 'Validate Foundry Core is kindled'
38	  zrbfc_sentinel
39	
40	  buc_log_args 'Verify Director RBRA file'
41	  test -n "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE:-}" || buc_die "RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE not set"
42	  test -f "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}"   || buc_die "GCB service env file not found: ${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}"
43	
44	  buc_log_args 'RBGJI inscribe step scripts (same Tools directory)'
45	  local z_self_dir="${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}"
46	  readonly ZRBFL_RBGJI_STEPS_DIR="${z_self_dir}/rbgji"
47	  test -d "${ZRBFL_RBGJI_STEPS_DIR}"   || buc_die "RBGJI steps directory not found: ${ZRBFL_RBGJI_STEPS_DIR}"
48	
49	  buc_log_args 'Define delete operation file prefix'
50	  readonly ZRBFL_DELETE_PREFIX="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbfl_delete_"
51	
52	  buc_log_args 'Define reliquary inscribe operation file prefix'
53	  readonly ZRBFL_RELIQUARY_PREFIX="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbfl_reliquary_"
54	
55	  readonly ZRBFL_KINDLED=1
56	}
57	
58	zrbfl_sentinel() {
59	  zrbfc_sentinel
60	  test "${ZRBFL_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module rbfl not kindled - call zrbfl_kindle first"
61	}
62	
63	######################################################################
64	# Internal Helpers (zrbfl_*)
65	
66	# Internal: Submit inscribe Cloud Build job.
67	# Single step: docker pull each upstream tool image, tag for GAR, push.
68	# Uses gcr.io/cloud-builders/docker as step image (always pullable — Google-hosted).
69	zrbfl_inscribe_submit() {
70	  zrbfl_sentinel
71	
72	  local -r z_token="${1:?Token required}"
73	  local -r z_reliquary="${2:?Reliquary datestamp required}"
74	
75	  buc_step "Constructing inscribe Cloud Build resource"
76	  local -r z_gar_host="${RBGD_GAR_LOCATION}${RBGC_GAR_HOST_SUFFIX}"
77	  local -r z_gar_path="${RBGD_GAR_PROJECT_ID}/${RBRR_GAR_REPOSITORY}"
78	  local -r z_mason_sa="projects/${RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}/serviceAccounts/${RBGD_MASON_EMAIL}"
79	
80	  # Inscribe step image: Google-hosted docker builder (always pullable, even under NO_PUBLIC_EGRESS)
81	  local -r z_step_image="gcr.io/cloud-builders/docker"
82	
83	  # Assemble inscribe step from script
84	  local -r z_script_path="${ZRBFL_RBGJI_STEPS_DIR}/rbgji01-inscribe-mirror.sh"
85	  test -f "${z_script_path}" || buc_die "Inscribe step script not found: ${z_script_path}"
86	
87	  local -r z_body_file="${ZRBFL_RELIQUARY_PREFIX}body.txt"
88	  local -r z_escaped_file="${ZRBFL_RELIQUARY_PREFIX}escaped.txt"
89	
90	  buc_log_args "Reading inscribe step script (skip shebang)"
91	  tail -n +2 "${z_script_path}" > "${z_body_file}" \
92	    || buc_die "Failed to read inscribe step script"
93	  local z_body=""
94	  z_body=$(<"${z_body_file}")
95	  test -n "${z_body}" || buc_die "Empty inscribe script body"
96	
97	  printf '#!/bin/bash\n%s' "${z_body}" > "${z_escaped_file}" \
98	    || buc_die "Failed to write escaped inscribe script body"
99	
100	  local -r z_step_file="${ZRBFL_RELIQUARY_PREFIX}step.json"
101	  echo "[]" > "${z_step_file}" || buc_die "Failed to initialize inscribe step JSON"
102	
103	  local -r z_step_built="${ZRBFL_RELIQUARY_PREFIX}step_built.json"
104	  jq \
105	    --arg name "${z_step_image}" \
106	    --arg id "inscribe-mirror" \
107	    --rawfile script "${z_escaped_file}" \
108	    '. + [{name: $name, id: $id, script: $script}]' \
109	    "${z_step_file}" > "${z_step_built}" \
110	    || buc_die "Failed to build inscribe step JSON"
111	  mv "${z_step_built}" "${z_step_file}" \
112	    || buc_die "Failed to finalize inscribe step JSON"
113	
114	  # Compose Build resource JSON
115	  buc_log_args "Composing inscribe Build resource JSON"
116	  local -r z_build_file="${ZRBFL_RELIQUARY_PREFIX}build.json"
117	
118	  jq -n \
119	    --slurpfile zjq_steps  "${z_step_file}" \
120	    --arg zjq_sa           "${z_mason_sa}" \
121	    --arg zjq_gar_host         "${z_gar_host}" \
122	    --arg zjq_gar_path         "${z_gar_path}" \
123	    --arg zjq_reliquaries_root "${RBGL_RELIQUARIES_ROOT}" \
124	    --arg zjq_reliquary        "${z_reliquary}" \
125	    --arg zjq_pool             "${RBDC_POOL_TETHER}" \
126	    --arg zjq_timeout          "${RBRR_GCB_TIMEOUT}" \
127	    '{
128	      steps: $zjq_steps[0],
129	      substitutions: {
130	        _RBGN_GAR_HOST:         $zjq_gar_host,
131	        _RBGN_GAR_PATH:         $zjq_gar_path,
132	        _RBGN_RELIQUARIES_ROOT: $zjq_reliquaries_root,
133	        _RBGN_RELIQUARY:        $zjq_reliquary
134	      },
135	      serviceAccount: $zjq_sa,
136	      options: {
137	        automapSubstitutions: true,
138	        logging: "CLOUD_LOGGING_ONLY",
139	        pool: { name: $zjq_pool }
140	      },
141	      timeout: $zjq_timeout
142	    }' > "${z_build_file}" \
143	    || buc_die "Failed to compose inscribe build JSON"
144	
145	  buc_log_args "Inscribe build JSON: ${z_build_file}"
146	
147	  buc_step "Submitting inscribe Cloud Build"
148	  rbgu_http_json "POST" "${ZRBFC_GCB_PROJECT_BUILDS_URL}" "${z_token}" \
149	    "reliquary_build_create" "${z_build_file}"
150	  rbgu_http_require_ok "Inscribe build submission" "reliquary_build_create"
151	
152	  local z_build_id=""
153	  z_build_id=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "reliquary_build_create" '.metadata.build.id') || z_build_id=""
154	  test -n "${z_build_id}" || buc_die "Build ID not found in builds.create response"
155	  echo "${z_build_id}" > "${ZRBFC_BUILD_ID_FILE}" || buc_die "Failed to persist build ID"
156	
157	  local -r z_console_url="${ZRBFC_CLOUD_QUERY_BASE};region=${RBGD_GCB_REGION}/${z_build_id}?project=${RBGD_GCB_PROJECT_ID}"
158	  buc_info "Inscribe build submitted: ${z_build_id}"
159	  buc_link "Click to " "Open build in Cloud Console" "${z_console_url}"
160	
161	  zrbfc_wait_build_completion 120 "Inscribe"  # ~10 minutes at 5s intervals (7 images to pull+push)
162	}
163	
164	######################################################################
165	# Public Functions (rbfl_*)
166	
167	rbfl_inscribe() {
168	  zrbfl_sentinel
169	
170	  buc_doc_brief "Inscribe a reliquary: mirror all tool images from upstream to a datestamped GAR namespace"
171	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
172	
173	  # Authenticate as Director
174	  buc_step "Loading Director RBRA credentials"
175	  source "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}" || buc_die "Failed to source Director RBRA"
176	
177	  buc_step "Authenticating as Director"
178	  local z_token=""
179	  z_token=$(rbgo_get_token_capture "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}") \
180	    || buc_die "Failed to get Director OAuth token"
181	
182	  # Compute reliquary datestamp: r + YYMMDDHHMMSS
183	  local -r z_reliquary="r${BURD_NOW_STAMP:2:6}${BURD_NOW_STAMP:9:6}"
184	  buc_info "Reliquary: ${z_reliquary}"
185	
186	  # Submit inscribe as a Cloud Build job (docker pull/tag/push on GCB)
187	  zrbfl_inscribe_submit "${z_token}" "${z_reliquary}"
188	
189	  buf_write_fact "${RBF_FACT_RELIQUARY}" "${z_reliquary}"
190	
191	  buc_success "Inscribe complete — reliquary ${z_reliquary} created"
192	  buc_info "Add RBRV_RELIQUARY=${z_reliquary} to vessel rbrv.env files to use this reliquary"
193	}
194	
195	rbfl_yoke() {
196	  zrbfl_sentinel
197	
198	  local -r z_stamp="${1:-}"
199	  local -r z_vessel="${2:-}"
200	
201	  buc_doc_brief "Yoke a reliquary stamp into a conjure vessel's rbrv.env — validate both, then rewrite RBRV_RELIQUARY"
202	  buc_doc_param "stamp"  "Reliquary datestamp (e.g., r260327172456)"
203	  buc_doc_param "vessel" "Vessel sigil (e.g., rbev-sentry-deb-tether)"
204	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
205	
206	  test -n "${z_stamp}"  || buc_die "Reliquary stamp required (arg 1)"
207	  test -n "${z_vessel}" || buc_die "Vessel sigil required (arg 2)"
208	
209	  buc_step "Validating vessel: ${z_vessel}"
210	  zrbfc_resolve_vessel "${z_vessel}"
211	  local -r z_vessel_dir=$(<"${ZRBFC_VESSEL_RESOLVED_DIR_FILE}")
212	  test -n "${z_vessel_dir}" || buc_die "Empty resolved vessel path"
213	  zrbfc_load_vessel "${z_vessel_dir}"
214	  local -r z_rbrv_file="${z_vessel_dir}/rbrv.env"
215	
216	  zrbrv_kindle
217	  zrbrv_enforce
218	
219	  case "${RBRV_VESSEL_MODE}" in
220	    conjure) ;;
221	    bind)    buc_die "Vessel '${z_vessel}' has mode=bind; Yoke only applies to conjure vessels. Bind vessels pin an upstream digest (no Cloud Build, no reliquary)." ;;
222	    graft)   buc_die "Vessel '${z_vessel}' has mode=graft; Yoke only applies to conjure vessels. Graft vessels push a locally-built image (no Cloud Build, no reliquary)." ;;
223	    *)       buc_die "Vessel '${z_vessel}' has unrecognized RBRV_VESSEL_MODE='${RBRV_VESSEL_MODE}'" ;;
224	  esac
225	  buc_info "Vessel valid — mode=${RBRV_VESSEL_MODE}, egress=${RBRV_EGRESS_MODE}"
226	
227	  buc_step "Authenticating as Director"
228	  local z_token=""
229	  z_token=$(rbgo_get_token_capture "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}") \
230	    || buc_die "Failed to get Director OAuth token"
231	
232	  buc_step "Validating reliquary stamp: ${z_stamp}"
233	  local -r z_rqy_subtree="${RBGL_RELIQUARIES_ROOT}/${z_stamp}/"
234	  local -r z_list_url="${ZRBFC_GAR_API_BASE}/${ZRBFC_GAR_PACKAGE_BASE}/packages?pageSize=1000"
235	  local -r z_list_infix="rbfl_yoke_list"
236	
237	  rbgu_http_json "GET" "${z_list_url}" "${z_token}" "${z_list_infix}"
238	  rbgu_http_require_ok "List reliquary packages" "${z_list_infix}"
239	
240	  local -r z_resp_file="${ZRBGU_PREFIX}${z_list_infix}${ZRBGU_POSTFIX_JSON}"
241	  local -r z_present_file="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbfl_yoke_present.txt"
242	
243	  jq -r --arg subtree "${z_rqy_subtree}" '
244	    .packages[]?.name
245	    | sub("^.*/packages/"; "")
246	    | gsub("%2F"; "/")
247	    | select(startswith($subtree))
248	    | ltrimstr($subtree)
249	  ' "${z_resp_file}" > "${z_present_file}" \
250	    || buc_die "Failed to extract reliquary package names"
251	
252	  local z_present=()
253	  local z_line=""
254	  while IFS= read -r z_line || test -n "${z_line}"; do
255	    test -n "${z_line}" || continue
256	    z_present+=("${z_line}")
257	  done < "${z_present_file}"
258	
259	  local -r z_expected=(
260	    "${RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_GCLOUD}"
261	    "${RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_DOCKER}"
262	    "${RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_ALPINE}"
263	    "${RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_SYFT}"
264	    "${RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_BINFMT}"
265	    "${RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_SKOPEO}"
266	  )
267	
268	  local z_missing=""
269	  local z_tool=""
270	  for z_tool in "${z_expected[@]}"; do
271	    local z_found=0
272	    local z_i=""
273	    for z_i in "${!z_present[@]}"; do
274	      test "${z_present[$z_i]}" = "${z_tool}" || continue
275	      z_found=1
276	      break
277	    done
278	    case "${z_found}" in
279	      1) ;;
280	      *) z_missing="${z_missing}${z_missing:+, }${z_tool}" ;;
281	    esac
282	  done
283	
284	  test -z "${z_missing}" || buc_die "Reliquary stamp '${z_stamp}' not found in Depot — expected 6 tool images under ${z_rqy_subtree}; missing: ${z_missing}. Re-run tt/rbw-dI.DirectorInscribesReliquary.sh to mint a fresh reliquary, or verify the stamp spelling."
285	  buc_info "Reliquary valid — all 6 tool images present (gcloud, docker, alpine, syft, binfmt, skopeo)"
286	
287	  buc_step "Yoking ${z_vessel} to reliquary ${z_stamp}"
288	
289	  local z_rbrv_lines=()
290	  local z_rbrv_line=""
291	  while IFS= read -r z_rbrv_line || test -n "${z_rbrv_line}"; do
292	    z_rbrv_lines+=("${z_rbrv_line}")
293	  done < "${z_rbrv_file}"
294	
295	  local -r z_tmp_file="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbfl_yoke_rbrv.env.new"
296	  : > "${z_tmp_file}" || buc_die "Failed to create ${z_tmp_file}"
297	
298	  local z_wrote=0
299	  local z_j=""
300	  for z_j in "${!z_rbrv_lines[@]}"; do
301	    case "${z_rbrv_lines[$z_j]}" in
302	      RBRV_RELIQUARY=*)
303	        printf 'RBRV_RELIQUARY=%s\n' "${z_stamp}" >> "${z_tmp_file}" \
304	          || buc_die "Failed to write RBRV_RELIQUARY line to ${z_tmp_file}"
305	        z_wrote=1
306	        ;;
307	      *)
308	        printf '%s\n' "${z_rbrv_lines[$z_j]}" >> "${z_tmp_file}" \
309	          || buc_die "Failed to write line to ${z_tmp_file}"
310	        ;;
311	    esac
312	  done
313	
314	  case "${z_wrote}" in
315	    1) ;;
316	    *) printf '\n# Tool Image Reliquary\nRBRV_RELIQUARY=%s\n' "${z_stamp}" >> "${z_tmp_file}" \
317	         || buc_die "Failed to append RBRV_RELIQUARY to ${z_tmp_file}" ;;
318	  esac
319	
320	  mv "${z_tmp_file}" "${z_rbrv_file}" || buc_die "Failed to finalize ${z_rbrv_file}"
321	
322	  buc_success "Yoked ${z_vessel} to reliquary ${z_stamp}"
323	  buc_info "Modified: ${z_rbrv_file}"
324	  buc_info "  RBRV_RELIQUARY=${z_stamp}"
325	  buc_info "Commit the change with your usual git workflow."
326	}
327	
328	rbfl_jettison() {
329	  zrbfl_sentinel
330	
331	  local z_locator="${1:-}"
332	  local z_force="${2:-}"
333	
334	  # Documentation block
335	  buc_doc_brief "Jettison an image tag from the registry by locator"
336	  buc_doc_param "locator" "Image locator in package-path:tag format (e.g. hallmarks/H/image:H)"
337	  buc_doc_param "--force" "Optional: skip confirmation prompt"
338	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
339	
340	  # Validate locator parameter
341	  test -n "${z_locator}" || buc_die "Locator parameter required (package-path:tag)"
342	
343	  # Parse locator into package path and tag
344	  case "${z_locator}" in
345	    *:*) : ;;
346	    *)   buc_die "Invalid locator format. Expected package-path:tag" ;;
347	  esac
348	  local z_pkg_path="${z_locator%:*}"
349	  local z_tag="${z_locator##*:}"
350	  test -n "${z_pkg_path}" || buc_die "Package path is empty in locator"
351	  test -n "${z_tag}" || buc_die "Tag is empty in locator"
352	
353	  # Check for --force flag
354	  local z_skip_confirm=false
355	  if test "${z_force}" = "--force"; then
356	    z_skip_confirm=true
357	  fi
358	
359	  buc_step "Authenticating as Director"
360	
361	  # Get OAuth token using Director credentials
362	  local z_token
363	  z_token=$(rbgo_get_token_capture "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}") || buc_die "Failed to get OAuth token"
364	
365	  # Confirm jettison unless --force
366	  if test "${z_skip_confirm}" = "false"; then
367	    buc_require "Will jettison: ${z_locator}" "yes"
368	  fi
369	
370	  buc_step "Jettisoning: ${z_locator}"
371	
372	  # Jettison by tag reference
373	  local z_status_file="${ZRBFL_DELETE_PREFIX}status.txt"
374	  local z_response_file="${ZRBFL_DELETE_PREFIX}response.json"
375	
376	  curl -X DELETE -s                                   \
377	    --connect-timeout "${RBCC_CURL_CONNECT_TIMEOUT_SEC}" \
378	    --max-time "${RBCC_CURL_MAX_TIME_SEC}"             \
379	    -H "Authorization: Bearer ${z_token}"             \
380	    -w "%{http_code}"                                 \
381	    -o "${z_response_file}"                           \
382	    "${ZRBFC_REGISTRY_API_BASE}/${RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX}${z_pkg_path}/manifests/${z_tag}" \
383	    > "${z_status_file}" || buc_die "DELETE request failed"
384	
385	  local z_http_code
386	  z_http_code=$(<"${z_status_file}")
387	  test -n "${z_http_code}" || buc_die "HTTP status code is empty"
388	
389	  if test "${z_http_code}" != "202" && test "${z_http_code}" != "204"; then
390	    local z_body="empty"
391	    if test -f "${z_response_file}"; then z_body=$(<"${z_response_file}"); fi
392	    buc_warn "Response body: ${z_body}"
393	    buc_die "Jettison failed with HTTP ${z_http_code}"
394	  fi
395	
396	  buc_success "Jettisoned or nonexistent: ${z_locator}"
397	}
398	
399	rbfl_abjure() {
400	  zrbfl_sentinel
401	
402	  local z_hallmark="${1:-}"
403	  local z_force="${2:-}"
404	
405	  # Documentation block
406	  buc_doc_brief "Abjure a hallmark — delete all GAR packages under hallmarks/<hallmark>/"
407	  buc_doc_param "hallmark" "Full hallmark (e.g., c260305133650-r260305160530)"
408	  buc_doc_param "--force" "Optional: skip confirmation prompt"
409	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
410	
411	  test -n "${z_hallmark}" || buc_die "Hallmark parameter required"
412	
413	  local z_skip_confirm=false
414	  if test "${z_force}" = "--force"; then
415	    z_skip_confirm=true
416	  fi
417	
418	  buc_step "Authenticating as Director"
419	  local z_token
420	  z_token=$(rbgo_get_token_capture "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}") || buc_die "Failed to get OAuth token"
421	
422	  # Enumerate packages under hallmarks/<hallmark>/ via GAR REST API.
423	  # Each immediate child of the subtree is one ark (image, vouch, pouch,
424	  # about, attest, diags). Iterating discovered children rather than a
425	  # hardcoded suffix list naturally tolerates graft's missing pouch.
426	  local -r z_subtree="${RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT}/${z_hallmark}/"
427	  buc_step "Enumerating packages under ${z_subtree}"
428	
429	  local -r z_list_url="${ZRBFC_GAR_API_BASE}/${ZRBFC_GAR_PACKAGE_BASE}/packages?pageSize=1000"
430	  local -r z_list_infix="rbfl_abjure_list"
431	
432	  rbgu_http_json "GET" "${z_list_url}" "${z_token}" "${z_list_infix}"
433	  rbgu_http_require_ok "List packages for abjure" "${z_list_infix}"
434	
435	  # GAR returns package names URL-encoded in the resource name (slashes as
436	  # %2F); decode and prefix-match to the hallmark subtree.
437	  local -r z_resp_file="${ZRBGU_PREFIX}${z_list_infix}${ZRBGU_POSTFIX_JSON}"
438	  local -r z_pkg_file="${ZRBFL_DELETE_PREFIX}packages.txt"
439	
440	  jq -r --arg subtree "${z_subtree}" '
441	    .packages[]?.name
442	    | sub("^.*/packages/"; "")
443	    | gsub("%2F"; "/")
444	    | select(startswith($subtree))
445	  ' "${z_resp_file}" > "${z_pkg_file}" \
446	    || buc_die "Failed to extract package names for hallmark subtree"
447	
448	  if ! test -s "${z_pkg_file}"; then
449	    buc_die "No packages found under ${z_subtree} — hallmark not present in registry"
450	  fi
451	
452	  local z_count
453	  z_count=$(wc -l < "${z_pkg_file}" | tr -d ' ')
454	
455	  # Confirm abjuration unless --force
456	  if test "${z_skip_confirm}" = "false"; then
457	    local z_confirm_msg="Will abjure ${z_count} packages under ${z_subtree}:"
458	    local z_pkg_path=""
459	    while IFS= read -r z_pkg_path || test -n "${z_pkg_path}"; do
460	      z_confirm_msg="${z_confirm_msg}\n  - ${z_pkg_path}"
461	    done < "${z_pkg_file}"
462	    buc_require "${z_confirm_msg}" "yes"
463	  fi
464	
465	  # Delete each package via GAR REST API.
466	  # DELETE returns a long-running operation; trust 200 as accepted (matches
467	  # the prior fire-and-forget semantics with 202/204 on the v2 manifest API).
468	  local z_pkg_path=""
469	  local z_del_idx=0
470	  while IFS= read -r z_pkg_path || test -n "${z_pkg_path}"; do
471	    buc_step "Deleting package: ${z_pkg_path}"
472	
473	    local z_pkg_encoded="${z_pkg_path//\//%2F}"
474	    local z_del_url="${ZRBFC_GAR_API_BASE}/${ZRBFC_GAR_PACKAGE_BASE}/packages/${z_pkg_encoded}"
475	    local z_del_infix="rbfl_abjure_del_${z_del_idx}"
476	
477	    rbgu_http_json "DELETE" "${z_del_url}" "${z_token}" "${z_del_infix}"
478	    rbgu_http_require_ok "Delete package ${z_pkg_path}" "${z_del_infix}"
479	
480	    buc_info "Deleted: ${z_pkg_path}"
481	    z_del_idx=$((z_del_idx + 1))
482	  done < "${z_pkg_file}"
483	
484	  echo ""
485	  buc_success "Hallmark abjured: ${z_hallmark} (${z_count} packages)"
486	}
487	
488	rbfl_tally() {
489	  zrbfl_sentinel
490	
491	  buc_doc_brief "Tally hallmarks with health status (vouched / pending / incomplete)"
492	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
493	
494	  buc_step "Authenticating as Retriever"
495	  test -f "${RBDC_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE}" \
496	    || buc_die "Retriever credential not found: ${RBDC_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE}"
497	  local z_token=""
498	  z_token=$(rbgo_get_token_capture "${RBDC_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE}") \
499	    || buc_die "Failed to get Retriever OAuth token"
500	
501	  buc_step "Enumerating hallmarks under ${RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT}/"
502	  zrbfc_list_packages_capture "${z_token}" "${RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT}"
503	
504	  if ! test -s "${ZRBFC_PACKAGE_LIST_FILE}"; then
505	    buc_info "No hallmarks found under ${RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT}/"
506	    buc_success "Tally complete — 0 hallmarks"
507	    return 0
508	  fi
509	
510	  # Load-then-iterate. A synthetic sentinel element appended to the array
511	  # lets the final hallmark flush through the same boundary branch as every
512	  # intermediate one (single flush site).
513	  local z_lines=()
514	  local z_line=""
515	  while IFS= read -r z_line || test -n "${z_line}"; do
516	    z_lines+=("${z_line}")
517	  done < "${ZRBFC_PACKAGE_LIST_FILE}"
518	  z_lines+=("__SENTINEL__ __SENTINEL__")
519	
520	  echo ""
521	  printf "  %-30s  %-11s  %s\n" "HALLMARK" "HEALTH" "BASENAMES"
522	  printf "  %-30s  %-11s  %s\n" "------------------------------" "-----------" "---------"
523	
524	  # State machine over <hallmark> <basename> pairs (file was sorted by the
525	  # capture helper). Vessel is no longer encoded in the GAR path —
526	  # restoration via about/vouch metadata is AAL territory.
527	  local z_prev_h="" z_prev_bns=""
528	  local z_prev_img=0 z_prev_abt=0 z_prev_vch=0
529	  local z_count=0 z_vouched_n=0 z_pending_n=0 z_incomplete_n=0
530	  local z_i="" z_h="" z_b="" z_health=""
531	
532	  for z_i in "${!z_lines[@]}"; do
533	    z_line="${z_lines[$z_i]}"
534	    test -n "${z_line}" || continue
535	
536	    z_h="${z_line%% *}"
537	    z_b="${z_line#* }"
538	    test -n "${z_h}" || continue
539	    test -n "${z_b}" || continue
540	
541	    if test "${z_h}" != "${z_prev_h}"; then
542	      if test -n "${z_prev_h}"; then
543	        if test "${z_prev_img}" = "1" \
544	          && test "${z_prev_abt}" = "1" \
545	          && test "${z_prev_vch}" = "1"; then
546	          z_health="vouched"
547	          z_vouched_n=$(( z_vouched_n + 1 ))
548	        elif test "${z_prev_img}" = "1" \
549	          && test "${z_prev_abt}" = "1"; then
550	          z_health="pending"
551	          z_pending_n=$(( z_pending_n + 1 ))
552	        else
553	          z_health="incomplete"
554	          z_incomplete_n=$(( z_incomplete_n + 1 ))
555	        fi
556	        printf "  %-30s  %-11s  %s\n" "${z_prev_h}" "${z_health}" "${z_prev_bns}"
557	        z_count=$(( z_count + 1 ))
558	      fi
559	
560	      case "${z_h}" in
561	        __SENTINEL__) break ;;
562	      esac
563	
564	      z_prev_h="${z_h}"
565	      z_prev_bns=""
566	      z_prev_img=0
567	      z_prev_abt=0
568	      z_prev_vch=0
569	    fi
570	
571	    z_prev_bns="${z_prev_bns}${z_prev_bns:+ }${z_b}"
572	    case "${z_b}" in
573	      "${RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_IMAGE}") z_prev_img=1 ;;
574	      "${RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_ABOUT}") z_prev_abt=1 ;;
575	      "${RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_VOUCH}") z_prev_vch=1 ;;
576	    esac
577	  done
578	
579	  echo ""
580	  buc_info "Total hallmarks: ${z_count}  (vouched: ${z_vouched_n}, pending: ${z_pending_n}, incomplete: ${z_incomplete_n})"
581	
582	  case "${z_pending_n}" in
583	    0) ;;
584	    *) buc_info "To vouch pending hallmarks:"
585	       buc_tabtarget "rbw-fV"
586	       ;;
587	  esac
588	
589	  case "${z_incomplete_n}" in
590	    0) ;;
591	    *) buc_info "To abjure incomplete hallmarks:"
592	       buc_tabtarget "rbw-fA"
593	       ;;
594	  esac
595	
596	  buc_success "Tally complete"
597	}
598	
599	rbfl_rekon_hallmark() {
600	  zrbfl_sentinel
601	
602	  local -r z_hallmark="${1:-}"
603	
604	  buc_doc_brief "List ark basenames present under a hallmark's GAR subtree"
605	  buc_doc_param "hallmark" "Hallmark identifier"
606	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
607	
608	  test -n "${z_hallmark}" || buc_die "Usage: rbw-irh <hallmark>"
609	
610	  buc_step "Authenticating as Director"
611	  test -f "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}" \
612	    || buc_die "Director credential not found: ${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}"
613	  local z_token=""
614	  z_token=$(rbgo_get_token_capture "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}") \
615	    || buc_die "Failed to get Director OAuth token"
616	
617	  buc_step "Enumerating arks under ${RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT}/${z_hallmark}/"
618	  zrbfc_list_packages_capture "${z_token}" "${RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT}"
619	
620	  # Filter the full hallmark enumeration to rows for this hallmark.
621	  local z_present=""
622	  local z_line=""
623	  local z_h=""
624	  local z_b=""
625	  while IFS= read -r z_line || test -n "${z_line}"; do
626	    test -n "${z_line}" || continue
627	    z_h="${z_line%% *}"
628	    z_b="${z_line#* }"
629	    if test "${z_h}" = "${z_hallmark}"; then
630	      z_present="${z_present}${z_present:+ }${z_b}"
631	    fi
632	  done < "${ZRBFC_PACKAGE_LIST_FILE}"
633	
634	  test -n "${z_present}" || buc_die "Hallmark not found: ${z_hallmark}"
635	
636	  echo ""
637	  printf "  %-10s  %-6s  %s\n" "BASENAME" "EXISTS" "PACKAGE-PATH"
638	  printf "  %-10s  %-6s  %s\n" "----------" "------" "------------"
639	
640	  local z_canon=""
641	  local z_mark=""
642	  local z_path=""
643	  for z_canon in \
644	    "${RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_IMAGE}" \
645	    "${RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_ABOUT}" \
646	    "${RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_VOUCH}" \
647	    "${RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_ATTEST}" \
648	    "${RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_POUCH}" \
649	    "${RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_DIAGS}"; do
650	    z_mark="no"
651	    case " ${z_present} " in
652	      *" ${z_canon} "*) z_mark="yes" ;;
653	    esac
654	    if test "${z_mark}" = "yes"; then
655	      z_path="${RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT}/${z_hallmark}/${z_canon}"
656	    else
657	      z_path="(absent)"
658	    fi
659	    printf "  %-10s  %-6s  %s\n" "${z_canon}" "${z_mark}" "${z_path}"
660	  done
661	
662	  echo ""
663	  buc_success "Rekon complete for ${z_hallmark}"
664	}
665

723	    else

733	rbfl_wrest() {
734	  zrbfl_sentinel
735	
736	  local -r z_locator="${1:-}"
737	
738	  buc_doc_brief "Wrest an image from the registry to local container runtime by locator"
739	  buc_doc_param "locator" "Image locator in package-path:tag format (e.g. hallmarks/H/image:H, reliquaries/r260327172456/syft:r260327172456, enshrines/eb-anchor:eb-anchor)"
740	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
741	
742	  test -n "${z_locator}" || buc_die "Locator parameter required (package-path:tag)"
743	
744	  case "${z_locator}" in
745	    *:*) : ;;
746	    *)   buc_die "Invalid locator format. Expected package-path:tag" ;;
747	  esac
748	  local -r z_pkg_path="${z_locator%:*}"
749	  local -r z_tag="${z_locator##*:}"
750	  test -n "${z_pkg_path}" || buc_die "Package path is empty in locator"
751	  test -n "${z_tag}"      || buc_die "Tag is empty in locator"
752	
753	  buc_step "Authenticating as Director"
754	  test -f "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}" \
755	    || buc_die "Director credential not found: ${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}"
756	  local z_token
757	  z_token=$(rbgo_get_token_capture "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}") \
758	    || buc_die "Failed to get OAuth token"
759	
760	  buc_step "Logging into container registry"
761	  local -r z_full_ref="${ZRBFC_REGISTRY_HOST}/${ZRBFC_REGISTRY_PATH}/${RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX}${z_locator}"
762	
763	  echo "${z_token}" | docker login -u oauth2accesstoken --password-stdin "https://${ZRBFC_REGISTRY_HOST}" \
764	    || buc_die "Container runtime authentication failed"
765	
766	  buc_step "Pulling image: ${z_full_ref}"
767	  docker pull "${z_full_ref}" || buc_die "Image pull failed"
768	
769	  local z_image_id
770	  docker inspect --format='{{.Id}}' "${z_full_ref}" > "${ZRBFC_SCRATCH_FILE}" 2>/dev/null \
771	    || buc_die "Failed to get image ID"
772	  z_image_id=$(<"${ZRBFC_SCRATCH_FILE}")
773	
774	  echo ""
775	  echo "Image wrested: ${z_full_ref}"
776	  echo "Local image ID: ${z_image_id}"
777	
778	  buc_success "Image wrest complete"
779	}

#!/bin/bash
